backend/main.mo: Motoko actor implementing the incident management backend with functions to create incidents, add updates, change status, delete resolved incidents, and query all or single incidents stored in a persistent map.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping two implemented features — dashboard keyword search/filter and admin panel delete-resolved-incident with confirmation — to their source files, components, and test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-to-two sentence summaries of every frontend source file, serving as a pre-built reference for the project's frontend structure and component responsibilities.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base CSS file defining light and dark OKLCH color token variables for shadcn/ui theming including background, card, border, and semantic colors.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point for the React app, mounting the app into a root div and loading the main TypeScript module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Sets up TanStack Router with a root route wrapping the Layout component, an index route for Dashboard, and an /admin route for AdminPanel.
frontend/src/components/AddUpdateForm.ts: React Hook Form-based form that lets admins select an existing incident and append a new timestamped update message via the useAddUpdate mutation.
frontend/src/components/ChangeStatusForm: Form component for selecting an incident and changing its status via a dropdown, displaying the current status for context before submission.
frontend/src/components/CreateIncidentFo: A React form component for creating new incidents, using react-hook-form for validation, checking for duplicate IDs, and submitting via a mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/IncidentCard.tsx: Displays a single incident as a collapsible card showing severity, status, affected service, timestamps, description, and a sorted list of updates.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Provides the app shell with a sticky header (navigation links, logout button), main content area, and footer with branding.
frontend/src/components/SeverityBadge.ts: Renders a styled badge for incident severity levels (critical, major, minor, informational) with color-coded dot indicators.
frontend/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx: Renders a styled badge for incident status values (investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved) with distinct color schemes.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) using TanStack Query, invalidating dependent queries when the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and provider, managing AuthClient lifecycle, login/logout flows, and persisting identity across page reloads.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for all incident-related backend operations including fetching, creating, updating, changing status, and deleting incidents, with automatic cache invalidation on mutations.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports fonts, sets up Tailwind directives, and defines CSS custom properties for light/dark theme colors including status and severity tokens using OKLCH color space.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrappers around the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/AdminPanel.tsx: Admin page that gates access behind Internet Identity login and provides tabbed UI for creating incidents, adding updates, changing status, and deleting resolved incidents.
frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx: Public status dashboard that displays active and resolved incidents with search filtering, severity-based sorting, and an overall system health banner.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration that maps OKLCH CSS variables to semantic color tokens (background, severity, status, sidebar, etc.) and registers typography, container-queries, and animate plugins.
project_state.json: Tracks the project state, version, architectural notes, known issues, and a list of existing implemented features for the district IT status page dashboard.
